texts.NASB95: And they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, He who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, He is baptizing and all are coming to Him.”
texts.NKJV: And they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, He who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified—behold, He is baptizing, and all are coming to Him!”
texts.DRB: And they came to John, and said to him: Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond the Jordan, to whom thou gavest testimony, behold he baptizeth, and all men come to him.

texts.NIV84: To this John replied, "A man can receive only what is given him from heaven.
texts.KJV: John answered and said, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.
texts.NASB95: John answered and said, “A man can receive nothing unless it has been given him from heaven.
texts.NKJV: John answered and said, “A man can receive nothing unless it has been given to him from heaven.
texts.DRB: John answered, and said: A man cannot receive any thing, unless it be given him from heaven.

texts.NIV84: You yourselves can testify that I said, 'I am not the Christ but am sent ahead of him.'
texts.KJV: Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.
texts.NASB95: You yourselves are my witnesses that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent ahead of Him.’
texts.NKJV: You yourselves bear me witness, that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent before Him.’
texts.DRB: You yourselves do bear me witness, that I said, I am not Christ, but that I am sent before him.

texts.NIV84: The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend who attends the bridegroom waits and listens for him, and is full of joy when he hears the bridegroom's voice. That joy is mine, and it is now complete.
texts.KJV: He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom's voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.
texts.NASB95: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. So this joy of mine has been made full.
texts.NKJV: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. Therefore this joy of mine is fulfilled.
texts.DRB: He that hath the bride, is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, who standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth with joy because of the bridegroom's voice. This my joy therefore is fulfilled.

texts.NIV84: He must become greater; I must become less.
texts.KJV: He must increase, but I must decrease.
texts.NASB95: He must increase, but I must decrease.
texts.NKJV: He must increase, but I must decrease.
texts.DRB: He must increase, but I must decrease.

texts.NIV84: "The one who comes from above is above all; the one who is from the earth belongs to the earth, and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all.
texts.KJV: He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.
texts.NASB95: “He who comes from above is above all, he who is of the earth is from the earth and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.
texts.NKJV: He who comes from above is above all; he who is of the earth is earthly and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.
texts.DRB: He that cometh from above, is above all. He that is of the earth, of the earth he is, and of the earth he speaketh. He that cometh from heaven, is above all.

texts.NIV84: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
texts.KJV: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.
texts.NASB95: What He has seen and heard, of that He testifies; and no one receives His testimony.
texts.NKJV: And what He has seen and heard, that He testifies; and no one receives His testimony.
texts.DRB: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth: and no man receiveth his testimony.

texts.NIV84: The man who has accepted it has certified that God is truthful.
texts.KJV: He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.
texts.NASB95: He who has received His testimony has set his seal to this, that God is true.
texts.NKJV: He who has received His testimony has certified that God is true.
texts.DRB: He that hath received his testimony, hath set to his seal that God is true.

texts.NIV84: For the one whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for God gives the Spirit without limit.
texts.KJV: For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
texts.NASB95: For He whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for He gives the Spirit without measure.
texts.NKJV: For He whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for God does not give the Spirit by measure.
texts.DRB: For he whom God hath sent, speaketh the words of God: for God doth not give the Spirit by measure.

texts.NIV84: The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in his hands.
texts.KJV: The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into his hand.
texts.NASB95: The Father loves the Son and has given all things into His hand.
texts.NKJV: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into His hand.
texts.DRB: The Father loveth the Son: and he hath given all things into his hand.

texts.NIV84: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life, but whoever rejects the Son will not see life, for God's wrath remains on him."
texts.KJV: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
texts.NASB95: He who believes in the Son has eternal life; but he who does not obey the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God abides on him.”
texts.NKJV: He who believes in the Son has everlasting life; and he who does not believe the Son shall not see life, but the wrath of God abides on him.”
texts.DRB: He that believeth in the Son, hath life everlasting; but he that believeth not the Son, shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.

texts.DRB: And you yourselves like to men who wait for their lord, when he shall return from the wedding; that when he cometh and knocketh, they may open to him immediately.

texts.NIV84: It will be good for those servants whose master finds them watching when he comes. I tell you the truth, he will dress himself to serve, will have them recline at the table and will come and wait on them.
texts.KJV: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them.
texts.NASB95: Blessed are those slaves whom the master will find on the alert when he comes; truly I say to you, that he will gird himself to serve, and have them recline at the table, and will come up and wait on them.
texts.NKJV: Blessed are those servants whom the master, when he comes, will find watching. Assuredly, I say to you that he will gird himself and have them sit down to eat, and will come and serve them.
texts.DRB: Blessed are those servants, whom the Lord when he cometh, shall find watching. Amen I say to you, that he will gird himself, and make them sit down to meat, and passing will minister unto them.

texts.NIV84: It will be good for those servants whose master finds them ready, even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night.
texts.KJV: And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants.
texts.NASB95: Whether he comes in the second watch, or even in the third, and finds them so, blessed are those slaves.
texts.NKJV: And if he should come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants.
texts.DRB: And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants.

texts.NIV84: But understand this: If the owner of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.
texts.KJV: And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through.
texts.NASB95: “But be sure of this, that if the head of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
texts.NKJV: But know this, that if the master of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched and not allowed his house to be broken into.
texts.DRB: But this know ye, that if the householder did know at what hour the thief would come, he would surely watch, and would not suffer his house to be broken open.

texts.NIV84: You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him."
texts.KJV: Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.
texts.NASB95: You too, be ready; for the Son of Man is coming at an hour that you do not expect.”
texts.NKJV: Therefore you also be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.”
texts.DRB: Be you then also ready: for at what hour you think not, the Son of man will come.

texts.NIV84: Peter asked, "Lord, are you telling this parable to us, or to everyone?"
texts.KJV: Then Peter said unto him, Lord, speakest thou this parable unto us, or even to all?
texts.NASB95: Peter said, “Lord, are You addressing this parable to us, or to everyone else as well?”
texts.NKJV: Then Peter said to Him, “Lord, do You speak this parable only to us, or to all people?”
texts.DRB: And Peter said to him: Lord, dost thou speak this parable to us, or likewise to all?

texts.NIV84: The Lord answered, "Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their food allowance at the proper time?
texts.KJV: And the Lord said, Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom his lord shall make ruler over his household, to give them their portion of meat in due season?
texts.NASB95: And the Lord said, “Who then is the faithful and sensible steward, whom his master will put in charge of his servants, to give them their rations at the proper time?
texts.NKJV: And the Lord said, “Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom his master will make ruler over his household, to give them their portion of food in due season?
texts.DRB: And the Lord said: Who (thinkest thou) is the faithful and wise steward, whom his lord setteth over his family, to give them their measure of wheat in due season?

texts.NIV84: It will be good for that servant whom the master finds doing so when he returns.
texts.KJV: Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.
texts.NASB95: Blessed is that slave whom his master finds so doing when he comes.
texts.NKJV: Blessed is that servant whom his master will find so doing when he comes.
texts.DRB: Blessed is that servant, whom when his lord shall come, he shall find so doing.

texts.NIV84: I tell you the truth, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
texts.KJV: Of a truth I say unto you, that he will make him ruler over all that he hath.
texts.NASB95: Truly I say to you that he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
texts.NKJV: Truly, I say to you that he will make him ruler over all that he has.
texts.DRB: Verily I say to you, he will set him over all that he possesseth.

texts.NIV84: But suppose the servant says to himself, 'My master is taking a long time in coming,' and he then begins to beat the menservants and maidservants and to eat and drink and get drunk.
texts.KJV: But and if that servant say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming; and shall begin to beat the menservants and maidens, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken;
texts.NASB95: But if that slave says in his heart, ‘My master will be a long time in coming,’ and begins to beat the slaves, both men and women, and to eat and drink and get drunk;
texts.NKJV: But if that servant says in his heart, ‘My master is delaying his coming,’ and begins to beat the male and female servants, and to eat and drink and be drunk,
texts.DRB: But if that servant shall say in his heart: My lord is long a coming; and shall begin to strike the menservants and maidservants, and to eat and to drink and be drunk:

texts.NIV84: The master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he is not aware of. He will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
texts.KJV: The lord of that servant will come in a day when he looketh not for him, and at an hour when he is not aware, and will cut him in sunder, and will appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.
texts.NASB95: the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know, and will cut him in pieces, and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
texts.NKJV: the master of that servant will come on a day when he is not looking for him, and at an hour when he is not aware, and will cut him in two and appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.
texts.DRB: The lord of that servant will come in the day that he hopeth not, and at the hour that he knoweth not, and shall separate him, and shall appoint him his portion with unbelievers.

texts.NIV84: "That servant who knows his master's will and does not get ready or does not do what his master wants will be beaten with many blows.
texts.KJV: And that servant, which knew his lord's will, and prepared not himself, neither did according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.
texts.NASB95: And that slave who knew his master’s will and did not get ready or act in accord with his will, will receive many lashes,
texts.NKJV: And that servant who knew his master’s will, and did not prepare himself or do according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.
texts.DRB: And that servant who knew the will of his lord, and prepared not himself, and did not according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.

texts.NIV84: But the one who does not know and does things deserving punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much, much will be demanded; and from the one who has been entrusted with much, much more will be asked.
texts.KJV: But he that knew not, and did commit things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few stripes. For unto whomsoever much is given, of him shall be much required: and to whom men have committed much, of him they will ask the more.
texts.NASB95: but the one who did not know it, and committed deeds worthy of a flogging, will receive but few. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required; and to whom they entrusted much, of him they will ask all the more.
texts.NKJV: But he who did not know, yet committed things deserving of stripes, shall be beaten with few. For everyone to whom much is given, from him much will be required; and to whom much has been committed, of him they will ask the more.
texts.DRB: But he that knew not, and did things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few stripes. And unto whomsoever much is given, of him much shall be required: and to whom they have committed much, of him they will demand the more.

blocks.title: Final End of Bride and Bridegroom

texts.NIV84: The light of a lamp will never shine in you again. The voice of bridegroom and bride will never be heard in you again. Your merchants were the world's great men. By your magic spell all the nations were led astray.
texts.KJV: And the light of a candle shall shine no more at all in thee; and the voice of the bridegroom and of the bride shall be heard no more at all in thee: for thy merchants were the great men of the earth; for by thy sorceries were all nations deceived.
texts.NASB95: and the light of a lamp will not shine in you any longer; and the voice of the bridegroom and bride will not be heard in you any longer; for your merchants were the great men of the earth, because all the nations were deceived by your sorcery.
texts.NKJV: The light of a lamp shall not shine in you anymore, and the voice of bridegroom and bride shall not be heard in you anymore. For your merchants were the great men of the earth, for by your sorcery all the nations were deceived.
texts.DRB: And the light of the lamp shall shine no more at all in thee; and the voice of the bridegroom and the bride shall be heard no more at all in thee: for thy merchants were the great men of the earth, for all nations have been deceived by thy enchantments.

texts.NIV84: On this mountain the LORD Almighty will prepare a feast of rich food for all peoples, a banquet of aged wine- the best of meats and the finest of wines.
texts.KJV: And in this mountain shall the Lord of hosts make unto all people a feast of fat things, a feast of wines on the lees, of fat things full of marrow, of wines on the lees well refined.
texts.NKJV:
And in this mountain
The Lord of hosts will make for all people
A feast of choice pieces,
A feast of wines on the lees,
Of fat things full of marrow,
Of well-refined wines on the lees.
texts.DRB: And the Lord of hosts shall make unto all people in this mountain, a feast of fat things, a feast of wine, of fat things full of marrow, of wine purified from the lees.

texts.NIV84: On this mountain he will destroy the shroud that enfolds all peoples, the sheet that covers all nations;
texts.KJV: And he will destroy in this mountain the face of the covering cast over all people, and the vail that is spread over all nations.
texts.NASB95:
And on this mountain He will swallow up the covering which is over all peoples,
Even the veil which is stretched over all nations.
texts.NKJV:
And He will destroy on this mountain
The surface of the covering cast over all people,
And the veil that is spread over all nations.
texts.DRB: And he shall destroy in this mountain the face of the bond with which all pie were tied, and the web that he over all nations.

texts.NIV84: he will swallow up death forever. The Sovereign LORD will wipe away the tears from all faces; he will remove the disgrace of his people from all the earth. The LORD has spoken.
texts.KJV: He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord God will wipe away tears from off all faces; and the rebuke of his people shall he take away from off all the earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.
texts.NASB95:
He will swallow up death for all time,
And the Lord God will wipe tears away from all faces,
And He will remove the reproach of His people from all the earth;
For the Lord has spoken.
texts.DRB: He shall cast death down headlong forever: and the Lord God shall wipe away tears from every face, and the reproach of his people he shall take away from off the whole earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.

texts.NIV84: The Spirit and the bride say, "Come!" And let him who hears say, "Come!" Whoever is thirsty, let him come; and whoever wishes, let him take the free gift of the water of life.
texts.KJV: And the Spirit and the bride say, Come. And let him that heareth say, Come. And let him that is athirst come. And whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely.
texts.NASB95: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come.” And let the one who hears say, “Come.” And let the one who is thirsty come; let the one who wishes take the water of life without cost.
texts.NKJV: And the Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” And let him who hears say, “Come!” And let him who thirsts come. Whoever desires, let him take the water of life freely.
texts.DRB: And the spirit and the bride say: Come. And he that heareth, let him say: Come. And he that thirsteth, let him come: and he that will, let him take the water of life, freely.

texts.NIV84: I warn everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: If anyone adds anything to them, God will add to him the plagues described in this book.
texts.KJV: For I testify unto every man that heareth the words of the prophecy of this book, If any man shall add unto these things, God shall add unto him the plagues that are written in this book:
texts.NASB95: I testify to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds to them, God will add to him the plagues which are written in this book;
texts.NKJV: For I testify to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: If anyone adds to these things, God will add to him the plagues that are written in this book;
texts.DRB: For I testify to every one that heareth the words of the prophecy of this book: If any man shall add to these things, God shall add unto him the plagues written in this book.

texts.NIV84: And if anyone takes words away from this book of prophecy, God will take away from him his share in the tree of life and in the holy city, which are described in this book.
texts.KJV: And if any man shall take away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God shall take away his part out of the book of life, and out of the holy city, and from the things which are written in this book.
texts.NASB95: and if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God will take away his part from the tree of life and from the holy city, which are written in this book.
texts.NKJV: and if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God shall take away his part from the Book of Life, from the holy city, and from the things which are written in this book.
texts.DRB: And if any man shall take away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God shall take away his part out of the book of life, and out of the holy city, and from these things that are written in this book.
